images.caption: Figure 1:Example agent trajectories, judge critiques, and human meta-annotations from Counsel.Each side represents a trajectory with multiple spans, where each box shows the output generation of a span that is conditioned on information in only preceding boxes.Left: A trajectory fromτ\tau-bench, a customer support benchmark. In this interaction an LLMJ (red) correctly flags the error location and reasons correctly about what the error is, hence, it is labeled as ”Spot on” by the meta-annotator (green).Right: A trajectory from DA-Code, a coding benchmark. In this interaction, an LLMJ incorrectly and prematurely flags a reasonable intermediate step by an agent, and is labeled as ”Should not have flagged” by the meta-annotator.

Figure 2:Judge critique ratesacross agents, benchmarks, and judge models. The figure shows the proportion of agent spans flagged as containing an error by each judge model, stratified by agent model forτ\tau-bench retail (left) and DA-Code (right).

Figure 3:Human meta-annotated quality of judge outputs. Proportion of critique and judgment labeled by human annotators as Spot On, Poor Reasoning (correct location), or Should Not Have Flagged, broken down by agent model, and judge model for each ofτ\tau-bench retail (left), and DA-Code (right).

Figure 6:Agent trajectory statistics across benchmarks and agent models.Top left: Distribution of the number of agent steps per trajectory onτ\tau-bench retail for GPT-OSS-20B and Qwen3 agents.Top right: Distribution of the number of agent steps per trajectory on DA-Code for Qwen3 agents.Bottom left: Distribution of the number of output tokens (not including reasoning) per agent step onτ\tau-bench retail for GPT-OSS-20B and Qwen3 agents.Bottom right: Distribution of the number of output tokens per agent step on DA-Code for the Qwen3 agent.

Figure 7:Analysis of self-preference bias in LLM-as-a-judge critiques.Proportion of agent spans critiqued by each judge model, grouped by whether the judge and agent belong to the same model family or different model families.pp-values for one-sided normal tests for proportions of whether the same-family judgments are less prevalent than different-family judgments are above their respective bars.

Figure 8:Effect of in-context meta-evaluation examples on agent performance.Ablations compare no feedback, only wrong location or poor-reasoning feedback, mixed feedback, and only spot-on feedback. Each point in a violin is the average task reward onτ\tau-bench retail across all 115 tasks in a benchmark run. There were 10 full benchmark runs for each violin.TopExamples provided to the agent’s system prompt.BottomExamples provided to an in-loop guardrail judge that may request a single retry.
